q.en: Why are the polar regions the coldest places on Earth?
q.zh: 为什么极地地区是地球上最冷的地方？

options.en: They are farthest from the Sun in space.
options.zh: 它们离太空中的太阳最远。

options.en: Sunlight hits at an angle there, so less heat is absorbed, and Earth is tilted.
options.zh: 阳光在那里是斜射的，吸收的热量更少，而且地球是倾斜的。

explain.en: Near the poles the Sun's rays hit at an angle and spread out, absorbing less heat; the tilt also leaves poles dark in winter.
explain.zh: 在两极附近，阳光斜射并分散，吸收的热量更少；地轴倾斜也使极地在冬季没有阳光。

options.en: Only in the northern half of the world
Only in the southern half of the world

explain.en: The book notes penguins live only in the south and polar bears only in the north, so they never meet.
explain.zh: 书里说企鹅只在南半球，北极熊只在北半球，所以它们永远不会相遇。

options.en: Permanently frozen soil that stays frozen all year
options.zh: 全年保持冻结的永久冻土层

explain.en: The glossary defines permafrost as a layer of soil in a cold region that stays frozen all year long.
explain.zh: 词汇表把永久冻土定义为寒冷地区全年保持冻结的土壤层。

q.en: Which animal can survive the Antarctic winter on land?
q.zh: 哪种动物能在南极大陆上熬过南极的冬天？

explain.en: Emperor penguins are the only creatures that can withstand the Antarctic winter on land.
explain.zh: 帝企鹅是唯一能在南极大陆上熬过冬天的生物。

q.en: How does a male emperor penguin care for the egg?
q.zh: 雄性帝企鹅如何照料企鹅蛋？

options.en: He leaves it on the ice alone.
He balances it on his feet, covers it, and huddles for warmth without eating for months.
options.zh: 把蛋放在脚上、盖住它，并挤在一起取暖，数月不吃不喝。

options.en: The female does all the care.
He buries it in the snow.

explain.en: The male balances the egg on his feet, covers it with a feathered flap, huddles with others, and eats nothing for months.
explain.zh: 雄性把蛋放在脚上、用带羽毛的皮褶盖住，与其他企鹅挤在一起取暖，并连续数月不吃不喝。

options.en: A cold, treeless Arctic region where the ground is always frozen
options.zh: 寒冷、无树的北极地区，地面常年冻结

explain.en: The glossary defines tundra as a cold, treeless Arctic region where the ground is always frozen.
explain.zh: 词汇表把苔原定义为寒冷、无树的北极地区，地面常年冻结。

options.en: Fat under the skin that keeps marine mammals warm
options.zh: 皮下脂肪，为海洋哺乳动物保暖

explain.en: The glossary defines blubber as fat under the skin that keeps marine mammals warm; penguins have a layer of it.
explain.zh: 词汇表把鲸脂定义为皮下的脂肪，为海洋哺乳动物保暖；企鹅也有一层。

q.en: Who are the native people of the Arctic region?
q.zh: 北极地区的原住民是谁？

explain.en: The Inuit are the native people of the Arctic region; they hunted caribou, seals, and whales.
explain.zh: 因纽特人是北极地区的原住民，他们捕猎驯鹿、海豹和鲸。

q.en: Why does the author say the Earth is warming up?
q.zh: 作者为什么说地球正在变暖？

options.en: To explain a threat to polar regions
options.zh: 为了说明极地面临的威胁

explain.en: The author explains warming melts permafrost and ice caps, threatening polar animals and the environment.
explain.zh: 作者说明变暖使永久冻土和冰盖融化，威胁极地动物和环境。

q.en: Why can't a polar bear and a penguin meet in nature?
q.zh: 为什么北极熊和企鹅在自然中不会相遇？

options.en: They live on opposite halves of the Earth.
options.zh: 它们生活在地球相对的两个半球。

options.en: One is a bird and one is a mammal.
options.zh: 一个是鸟，一个是哺乳动物。

explain.en: Penguins live only in the southern half and polar bears only in the northern half, so a drawing of both together is impossible in real life.
explain.zh: 企鹅只在南半球，北极熊只在北半球，所以现实中两者同框是不可能的。

prompt.en: Choose one polar animal from the book (such as the polar bear, emperor penguin, or caribou) and explain how it has adapted to survive the cold.
prompt.zh: 从书中选一种极地动物（如北极熊、帝企鹅或驯鹿），说明它如何适应寒冷环境而生存。

en: Name one body feature that helps it stay warm.
zh: 说出一种帮助它保暖的身体特征。
